figures.caption: Fig. 3: Guidance strength in reverse diffusion (tstart = 0.3 and α = 2.0).

Fig. 1: The overall framework of AeroTrajGen. The training phase involves learning an obstacle-aware diffusion transformer from expert demonstrations, while the inference phase incorporates CBF-guided sampling to ensure safety during trajectory generation. The reverse diffusion process (RDP) is modified by integrating the safety gradient derived from the control barrier function (CBF), guiding the generated trajectories away from unsafe regions.
TABLE IV: Trajectory Quality Comparison (Config C)

Fig. 4: Maneuver styles demonstration with CBF-guided safe trajectory generation. The dashed green trajectories represent the CBF-guided approach, while the dashed red trajectories represent the unguided approach. The solid magenta trajectories represent the ground truth. Obstacles are shown as red spheres. The CBF-guided trajectories successfully avoid collisions while the unguided trajectories often intersect with obstacles, demonstrating the effectiveness of our safety-guided diffusion sampling method.
Fig. 2: Comparison of different safety probability functions (σ = 0.5).
